The following is a list of test IDs that cover the requirement:

- T-6, T-9, T-15, T-17, T-18, T-20, T-21

These tests cover the requirement by verifying that the system documentation describes the databases and database versions used, verifying that encryption keys are managed, verifying that switching on and off and status of switches is possible, verifying that the system can record and store information when high power exceeds the limit value per phase, and configuring records to be stored for events. Additionally, the system must comply with various legal requirements such as the Swedish Energy Markets Inspectorate's Regulations and General Advice on Metering, Calculation and Reporting of Transmitted Electricity, the Swedish Regulations on Measuring Instruments, the EU MID Directive 2014/32/EU, EU Regulation 2016/679, EU Declaration of Conformity, and EU Regulation 2016/2 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data. The system must also meet the requirements for Inspection according to Regulation STAFS 2009:8, and be certified for Category 1 and 2 meters according to method SPM 1618, test with 12kV surge voltage, and conform to various standards such as SS-EN61869-1, SS-EN61869-2, SS-EN50470 and IEC 62056 (DLMS/COSEM suite). The system must also be able to export energy values automatically and manually, and have a common information model (CIM) for the exchange of information between systems.

However, based on the information provided, it appears that the requirement "R-8" is related to energy values being exported within 30 seconds, and the tests "T-0" through "T-18" seem to be related to various aspects of metering and reporting of transmitted electricity. The tests "T-19" and "T-20" appear to be related to legal requirements, while "T-21" and "T-22" seem to be related to the physical characteristics of the metering unit. The tests "T-23" and "T-24" seem to be related to the durability of the labeling on the metering unit. The tests "T-25" and "T-26" seem to be related to the design of the metering solution. The tests "T-27" and "T-28" seem to be related to automatic and manual export of energy values. The test "T-29" seems to be related to the Common Information Model (CIM).

The following is a list of test IDs that cover the requirement "HES should be able to handle integrations via files":

- T-6
- T-15
- T-16
- T-17
- T-18
- T-20
- T-21
- T-23
- T-24
- T-25
- T-26
- T-27
- T-28
- T-30

These tests cover the requirement by verifying that the system documentation describes the databases and database versions used, and that the solution description details how encryption keys are managed, among other things. Additionally, tests T-11 and T-12 require the user to filter and sort the alarm list, and tests T-13 and T-14 involve generating faults in the meter to test the system's response. Test T-19 requires the user to set a low limit value in the meter to trigger an event, and tests T-20 and T-21 involve generating high power or voltage to cause an event in the meter. Test T-22 requires the user to configure records to be stored for events, and tests T-23 and T-24 involve verifying that the labeling of the meter's manufacturing number is durable and non-removable. Test T-25 and T-26 require the user to review the design of the solution, and test T-27 involves waiting for the time for export to occur. Test T-28 requires the user to export energy values manually, and test T-29 involves examining the content of messages from HES.

I cannot directly analyze the given data as it is in a format that is not easily readable by humans. However, based on the information provided, it appears that the requirement "Configure records to be stored for events" is covered by tests "T-19" and "T-18". These tests involve generating events in the meter and verifying that the records are stored in the Metering Unit. Additionally, the requirement "Send the same info again" is covered by test "T-6". This test involves receiving the same information from both the file and the meter. The requirement "View last measurement value and time" is covered by test "T-13". This test involves verifying that the last measurement value and time of communication with the Metering Unit are displayed in HES. The requirement "Metering unit display - Cat1 1 phase" is covered by tests "T-14" and "T-15". These tests involve verifying that the display of the Metering Unit for each phase can show the instantaneous value of voltage, current, and power for the extraction and input of electricity. The requirement "Metering unit display - Cat1 3 phase" is covered by tests "T-16" and "T-17". These tests are similar to those for Cat1 1 phase but cover the display of voltage, current, and power for each phase in a 3-phase system. The requirement "Metering unit display - Cat2" is covered by tests "T-7" and "T-8". These tests involve preparing test cases based on the agreed requirements and verifying that the test report contains at least the required information. The requirement "Data in HES" is covered by test "T-9". This test involves verifying that structure data can be handled in HES, including facility ID, metering unit, metrics, and events. The requirement "Events to alert list" is covered by test "T-10". This test involves resetting the clock in the meter and triggering a self-diagnosis and generating a fault during the diagnosis itself, disconnecting the power on one phase, and connecting production to the meter and the measuring point in HES. The requirement "Cumulative calculator" is covered by test "T-11". This test involves verifying that the metering unit is equipped with a cumulative counter and displays at least 7 integer digits and 3 decimal digits. The requirement "Metering unit display - Cat1 1 phase" and "Metering unit display - Cat1 3 phase" are covered by tests "T-14" and "T-15" respectively. These tests are similar to those for Cat1 1 phase and Cat1 3 phase but cover the display of voltage, current, and power for each phase in a 1-phase and 3-phase system. The requirement "Metering unit display - Cat2" is covered by test "T-7". This test involves preparing test cases based on the agreed requirements and verifying that the test report contains at least the required information. The requirement "Content of the test report" is covered by test "T-8". This test involves verifying that the test report contains at least the following information: test cases executed, results of the tests, residual errors, if any, residual test cases, if any, suggested actions. The requirement "Legal requirements" is covered by tests "T-20" to "T-23". These tests involve verifying that the Metering System meets various legal requirements, including Ellag SFS 1997:857, The Ordinance (SFS 1999:716), EU MID Directive 2014/32/EU, EU Regulation 2016/679, Extended type approval and certification of Category 1 and 2 meters according to method SPM 1618, DLMS/COSEM suite, and EU Regulation 2016/679 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data. The requirement "Cat 2 shall meet the requirements for control" is covered by test "T-21". This test involves verifying that Metering Units Category 2 meet the requirements for Inspection, according to Regulation STAFS 2009:8. The requirement "Resistant marking - Cat1" and "Resistant marking - Cat2" are covered by tests "T-22" and "T-23". These tests involve verifying that the labeling of the Meter's manufacturing number is durable for the lifetime of the Meter, i.e. no removable tape or print that can be scraped off. The requirement "Location of the marking - Cat1" and "Location of the label - Cat2" are covered by tests "T-24" and "T-25". These tests involve verifying that the Meter's serial number is placed on a non-removable part, not on connector covers or Communication Module covers. The requirement "Design of the solution" is covered by test "T-26". This test involves reviewing the installed solution and comparing it with the requirements in 2.1 Technical solution. The requirement "Automatic export of energy values" is covered by test "T-28". This test involves waiting until the time for export occurs. The requirement "Manual export of energy values" is covered by test "T-29". This test involves starting an export from HES. The requirement "Common Information Model (CIM)" is covered by test "T-30". This test involves examining the content of the messages from HES.

I cannot provide a definitive answer without analyzing the specific requirement and tests you've provided. However, based on the information given, it appears that tests T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8, T-9, T-10, T-11, T-12, T-13, T-14, T-15, T-16, T-17, T-18, T-19, T-20, T-21, T-22, T-23, T-24, T-25, T-26, T-27, T-28, T-29, T-30] are all tests that cover the requirement "Send the same info again". The requirement states that the system should be able to send the same information to HES several times, and the tests listed in the input meet this requirement by verifying that the system documentation describes the databases and database versions used, verifying that encryption keys are managed, verifying that switching on and off and status of switches is possible, measuring performance during normal reporting operation, putting the import file in the import folder, preparing test cases based on agreed requirements, verifying that structure data can be handled in HES, verifying that events can be handled in HES, verifying that the last measurement value and time of communication with the Metering Unit are displayed in HES, recording when high power exceeds limit value per phase, configuring records to be stored for events, fulfilling legal requirements, and meeting the requirements for control for Metering Units Category 2. Additionally, tests T-26, T-27, T-30 may cover the requirement if they test the design of the solution, automatic export of energy values, or common information model (CIM) respectively. However, without further context or information, it is impossible to definitively determine which tests cover the requirement without analyzing the specific requirement and tests in detail.
